title: The Settlement Replay Claim
alert: A replayed settlement event can create duplicate external deliveries.

briefing.objective: Prevent a re-entrant settlement replay from delivering the same event twice without blocking independent events.
briefing.successCriterion: The Python unittest suite must show exactly one external delivery for a re-entrant replay while different settlement events still deliver.
briefing.rootCause: The service performs the external delivery before it records ownership of the settlement event.
briefing.evidence: The acceptance suite re-enters the service while the first delivery is in progress and compares the delivery log with the claim ledger.

telemetry.impact: Duplicate settlement notices can trigger repeated downstream payouts and reconciliation work.

events.message: External delivery count exceeded unique settlement events.
Settlement set-204 was replayed during an active delivery.
The same settlement was observed twice before a claim record existed.

stakeholderMessages.author: Lena - Finance
stakeholderMessages.body: Finance paused the payout batch. We must prevent a repeated settlement notice without suppressing a different settlement event.

stakeholderMessages.author: Maya - PM
stakeholderMessages.body: Can we contain duplicate settlement notices before the payout deadline without dropping a valid replay?

stakeholderMessages.author: Ash - On-call
stakeholderMessages.body: A replay re-entered while set-204 was inside the delivery callback. The delivery count increased before the claim ledger did.

stakeholderMessages.author: Jon - Senior Eng
stakeholderMessages.body: Which durable state must exist before an external side effect can trigger a replay?

stakeholderMessages.author: AI Pair
stakeholderMessages.body: I prepared three replay changes. Compare the claim ledger with the delivery boundary before selecting one.
